name: Chicken Tuscany
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon Coarsely ground black pepper, divided
1 cup Homemade or canned chicken broth
1 1/2 cups Butter, cut into small pieces (3 sticks)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small flat dish, sprinkle half the oregano, thyme, rosemary, garlic, and black pepper.
2. Place the chicken breast halves in a single layer on top of the herbs.
3. Pour 4 tablespoons olive oil over the chicken.
4. Sprinkle the remaining herbs, garlic, and pepper over the chicken breasts.
5. Cover and refrigerate for at least 3 hours or overnight.
6. While the chicken is marinating, prepare the Marsala reduction:
a. In a saucepan, heat 1 1/2 teaspoons olive oil.
b. Add shallots and sauté until tender.
c. Add the Marsala and chicken broth and stir to scrape up any brown bits from the bottom of the pan.
d. Bring to a simmer and cook until reduced by half.
e. Skim off excess fat and strain to remove shallots. Set aside.
7. Grill or broil chicken breasts for about 7 to 8 minutes per side, or until done.
8. While the chicken is cooking, heat a sauté pan.
9. Add the Marsala reduction, mushrooms, tomatoes, and green onions. Bring to a simmer and reduce by half.
10. Remove from heat and stir the butter into the sauce bit by bit, stirring constantly, until the sauce is slightly thickened.
11. Serve the chicken breasts topped with the sauce.
Makes 4 servings.
